briefdescription.para: Mutex to avoid concurrent access.
Number of preallocated message (PoolElem) objects.

detaileddescription.para: Statically allocated memory pool for messages of type PoolElem

briefdescription.para: Number of preallocated buffers of fixed size 32.

detaileddescription.para: Statically allocated memory pool for 32 byte fixed size buffer

briefdescription.para: Number of preallocated buffers of fixed size 128.

detaileddescription.para: Statically allocated memory pool for 128 byte fixed size buffer

briefdescription.para: Number of preallocated buffers of fixed size 256.

detaileddescription.para: Statically allocated memory pool for 256 byte fixed size buffer

briefdescription.para: Number of preallocated buffers of fixed size 512.

detaileddescription.para: Statically allocated memory pool for 512 byte fixed size buffer

briefdescription.para: Number of preallocated buffers of fixed size 1024.

detaileddescription.para: Statically allocated memory pool for 1024 byte fixed size buffer

briefdescription.para: MsgPool class destructor.

detaileddescription.para: The destructor is never called and is implemented only for completion reasons. It simply triggers a debug message on info debug level.

briefdescription.para: Function to allocate a message of type PoolElem from message pool.

detaileddescription.para:
minSize
The minimal size of the message buffer
A pointer to the allocated message of type PoolElem
The function lock mutex lock to avoid concurrent access and allocate a message by calling function alloc of the message pool elemPool of class MemoryPool. According to the minimal size of the message buffer minSize the buffer will be allocated from one of the fixed sized buffer pools and the member variables buf and bufLen of the PoolElem class are set accordingly. Finally the member variables of the PoolElem class are assigned. Variable dataLen to 0, variable pool with this pointer of MsgPool instance and variable inPool to false before the pointer to the message of type PoolElem will be returned.

briefdescription.para: Getter function to allocate a message of type PoolElem from message pool.

detaileddescription.para:
A pointer to the allocated message of type PoolElem
The function allocate a message by calling member function getNewElem with paramter minSize = 0 and return the pointer to the allocated message.

briefdescription.para: Function to free (release) a message of type PoolElem to the message pool.

detaileddescription.para:
elem
Pointer to the allocated message of type PoolElem
The function lock mutex lock to avoid concurrent access. According to the size of the buffer the message buffer will be released to the fixed size buffer it belongs to by calling the fixed size pools free method. Afterwards the message will be released by cvalling the message pool method free and variable inPool will be set to true.

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the total number of messages in the pool.

detaileddescription.para:
Total number of messages in the pool
The function returns the total number of messages in the pool

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the number of used messages of the pool.

detaileddescription.para:
The number of used messages of the pool
The function returns the number of used messages of the pool

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the maximum number of used messages of the pool.

detaileddescription.para:
The number of maximal used messages of the pool
The function returns the number of maximal used messages of the pool

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the number of available messages of the pool.

detaileddescription.para:
The number of available messages of the pool
The function returns the number of available messages of the pool

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the total number of buffer memory in one of the buffer pools.

detaileddescription.para:
size
Size of the buffer
Total number of buffers in one of the buffer pools
The function returns the total number of buffers in one of the buffer pools

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the number of used buffer memory in one of the buffer pools.

detaileddescription.para:
size
Size of the buffer
Number of used buffers in one of the buffer pools
The function returns the number of used buffers in one of the buffer pools

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the maximum number of used buffer memory in one of the buffer pools.

detaileddescription.para:
size
Size of the buffer
Number of maximal used buffers in one of the buffer pools
The function returns the maximalnumber of used buffers in one of the buffer pools

briefdescription.para: Getter function to retrieve the number of available buffer memory in one of the buffer pools.

detaileddescription.para:
size
Size of the buffer
Number of available buffers in one of the buffer pools
The function returns the number of available buffers in one of the buffer pools

briefdescription.para: MsgPool status function.

detaileddescription.para: This function print out statistics of the all allocated memory pools.

briefdescription.para: The inst function to retrieve the exclusiv only instance of the class.

detaileddescription.para:
Returns a pointer to the class instance
In case there is no instance created, the constructor will be called to create the instance. Finally a pointer to the instance is returned.

briefdescription.para: MsgPool class constructor.

detaileddescription.para: The constructor simply triggers a debug message on info debug level.

briefdescription.para: RTOS wrapper for message pool support implemented according to C++ singleton design pattern.

simplesect.title: Detailed Description
simplesect.para: The MsgPool class implements a C++ wrapper for message pool support using the kernel memory slab API provided by the Zephyr operating system. See details in Zephyr Memory Slab documentation or in API documentation. The MsgPool class provides methods to allocate messages from the pool and to release (free) them back into the pool. Furthermore it provides a method to count the number of objects in the queue and to print statistics.
